name: JSONVault Dashboard UI Guide
purpose: Define the dashboard product scope, UX rules, visual language, safety constraints, and implementation guardrails for jsonvault-ui.

source_of_truth.rule: When this file disagrees with jsonvault-core behavior or the docs, core and docs win. Update this guide after the API contract changes.

product_direction.must_not_copy: Do not clone another product's brand, exact layout, icon set decisions, or component styling.

important_core_behaviors: Databases and collections can be lazily created by writes when the token allows the path.
Documents support ETags for optimistic concurrency.
List requests support limit, offset, filter, sort, and search query parameters.
SSE streams document changes for realtime app features.
Transient publish broadcasts events without persisting them for replay.
Indexes and FTS configuration can run as background operations.
Webhook delivery is protected by SSRF checks and delivery records.
Admin/root operations are separate from project and runtime operations.

realtime_model.plain_language: JSONVault realtime uses Server-Sent Events. A client opens one long-lived HTTP connection to a collection, and JSONVault streams document change events over that connection as inserts, updates, and deletes happen.
main_flow: The app authenticates with a token that can read the target database and collection.
JSONVault keeps the HTTP response open as text/event-stream.
When a document is inserted, updated, or deleted, JSONVault writes an SSE event to subscribed clients.
Each committed document event includes a sequence ID. Clients save the latest sequence they processed.
If the connection drops, the client reconnects with Last-Event-ID or last_event_id to replay retained committed document events.
JSONVault also sends keepalive comments so proxies are less likely to close idle streams.

event_types.insert: A new document was created and the event includes document data and ETag.
event_types.update: An existing document changed and the event includes the latest document data and ETag.
event_types.delete: A document was deleted and the event includes the document ID.
event_types.transient_publish: A temporary JSON message broadcast to active subscribers. It is not saved and cannot be replayed.

dashboard_purpose: Show developers what their app will receive from live subscriptions.
Let developers confirm document changes arrive without polling.
Show active listener count through presence.
Let developers send a temporary test event while building live UI.
realtime_model.browser_auth_note: Native browser EventSource cannot set custom Authorization headers. If JSONVault requires Bearer auth for subscribe requests, jsonvault-ui should use a fetch-based SSE client or a trusted backend proxy instead of putting secrets in query strings.
what_it_is_not: It is not a replacement for stored documents.
It is not a durable message queue.
Transient publish events are not replayed after reconnect.
It should not be shown as connected unless the UI has a real open stream.

app_developer.description: Builds application code that talks to JSONVault with read_only or read_write runtime keys.

project_owner.description: Manages one database/project through a project_admin token or equivalent trusted dashboard backend session.
primary_needs: mint scoped runtime keys when permitted

host_operator.description: Runs the JSONVault server and can perform root server operations.

runtime_keys.read_only: Read documents and safe metadata within database and collection constraints.
runtime_keys.read_write: Read and write documents, use transactions, subscribe to realtime, read presence, and publish transient messages within constraints.
rules: Runtime keys are the only keys suitable for normal app clients.
Runtime keys must not expose management actions such as index builds, FTS rebuilds, schema mutation, webhook configuration, backup, or key minting.
Project management tokens are for trusted dashboard, backend, or CLI workflows.
Do not place project management tokens in ordinary browser or mobile app runtime code.
The UI must drive visible actions from the token capabilities returned by GET /api/v1/me.
Root admin controls server-wide operations.
Never expose the root admin key to normal project users or runtime app code.
Admin-only UI must be visually and structurally separated from project screens.
admin_only_features: global webhook delivery inspection and retry

human_auth.status: dashboard-layer feature, not jsonvault-core account API
rules: Keep dashboard user sessions separate from JSONVault API tokens.
Do not ask normal users for JSONVAULT_JWT_SECRET or encryption keys.
Only self-hosted operator setup may ask for a root admin key, and it must be masked and treated as a secret.
Generated JWTs should be shown once with copy actions, then redacted.
Store dashboard session state separately from runtime and management credentials.
Never store JSONVAULT_JWT_SECRET in browser-accessible state.
Never log secret tokens or admin keys.

visual_language.density: medium-dense, optimized for repeated developer work

layout.desktop: Fixed sidebar, top project/database context, main work area, optional right details panel.
layout.tablet: Sidebar may collapse; secondary panels become drawers.
layout.mobile: Prioritize browse, inspect, and essential edit flows. Use full-screen JSON editing when needed.

gradients.rule: Do not use gradient backgrounds, gradient blobs, gradient orbs, bokeh, or purple-blue gradient hero sections.
gradients.allowed_exception: A small semantic status accent may use a flat tint. Avoid decorative gradients entirely.

rules: Do not put cards inside cards.
Do not style page sections as floating decorative cards.
Prefer tables, panels, split views, and drawers for dashboard work.
Use green as a restrained database/action accent, not as a full-page theme.
Use semantic colors only for semantic meaning.
Avoid palettes dominated by purple, beige, dark slate, brown, orange, or espresso tones.

typography.sans_stack: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, Segoe UI, sans-serif
typography.mono_stack: JetBrains Mono, SFMono-Regular, Consolas, Liberation Mono, monospace
rules: Use compact headings inside dashboard panels.
Use monospace for IDs, routes, ETags, JWT IDs, JSON, errors, and code.
Do not scale font size with viewport width.
Letter spacing should be 0 except slight positive tracking on all-caps micro labels.
Use lucide icons for common actions when available.
Icon-only buttons need tooltips and accessible labels.
Do not hand-draw SVG icons when a standard icon exists.

visibility_rules.Overview: Visible when the dashboard has a valid project context.
visibility_rules.Documents: Visible when documents:read is present or the scope is read_only, read_write, project_admin, or admin.
visibility_rules.Collections: Visible when metadata:read, collections:manage, project_admin, or admin is present.
visibility_rules.Indexes: Visible when metadata:read, indexes:manage, project_admin, or admin is present.
visibility_rules.Search: Visible when metadata:read, fts:manage, project_admin, or admin is present.
visibility_rules.Schemas: Visible when metadata:read, schemas:manage, project_admin, or admin is present.
visibility_rules.Webhooks: Visible when webhooks:manage, project_admin, or admin is present.
visibility_rules.Operations: Visible when operations:read, project_admin, or admin is present.
visibility_rules.API Keys: Visible when keys:manage or root admin is present.
visibility_rules.Realtime: Visible when document read access is present.
visibility_rules.Admin: Visible only for root admin features.

realtime: Last-Event-ID replay for retained committed document events

unsupported_or_out_of_scope: editing server environment variables from the dashboard
online restore upload from the dashboard
persistent operation history across server restart
persistent audit history across server restart unless core adds it
schema dry-run against all existing documents
webhook secret rotation endpoint unless core adds it
webhook test delivery endpoint unless core adds it
cursor pagination unless core adds it
visual query planner with guaranteed index recommendations
team invitations unless implemented by a dashboard auth layer

login.purpose: Authenticate a human user into the dashboard app.
content: server or workspace context when applicable
rules: Use a plain auth panel.
Do not use a marketing hero or gradient background.
Do not mention the root admin key in normal login.

register.purpose: Create a dashboard user account when the dashboard auth layer supports it.
register.next_step: Send the user to project onboarding or server connection.
rules: Do not ask for JSONVAULT_JWT_SECRET.
Do not ask for encryption keys.
Do not ask for root admin key in normal registration.

connect_server.purpose: Connect jsonvault-ui or its backend to a JSONVault core server.
content: operator-only root admin key input when self-hosted
rules: Mask root admin key input.
Explain operator-only scope.
Never reveal the saved key.

overview.purpose: Summarize the selected database/project status without fake metrics.
overview.empty_state: Show setup actions based on actual capabilities.

documents.purpose: Browse, inspect, create, edit, and delete JSON documents safely.
safety: Use If-Match for updates and deletes when an ETag is available.
Do not silently coerce invalid JSON.
Explain that collection names are API path segments.

guidance: Explain that indexes help repeated filters.
Warn that builds may run in the background.
Do not imply every query needs an index.

search.purpose: Configure and test full-text searchable fields.
guidance: Explain that broad terms can hit query budgets.
Show empty state when no FTS fields are configured.

schemas.purpose: Validate and enforce document shape with JSON Schema.
safety: Warn that schema changes affect future writes.

webhooks.purpose: Configure outbound event integrations for document changes.
safety: Warn that localhost, private IPs, and redirects are blocked by SSRF protection.
Do not reveal old webhook secrets.
Show delivery status and retry results clearly.

rules: Poll with backoff.
Do not claim completion until the API reports completion.
Explain temporary operation history if applicable.

api_keys.purpose: Create safe runtime keys and trusted project keys when permitted.

key_types.read_write: For runtime app CRUD, transactions, realtime, and publish.
key_types.project_admin: For trusted dashboard, backend, or CLI management workflows only.

safety: Do not show JWT signing secret.
Do not suggest putting project_admin keys in app clients.

realtime.purpose: Help developers verify what app clients receive from live document subscriptions.
use_cases: confirm inserts, updates, and deletes arrive without polling
test reconnect and Last-Event-ID replay behavior
send temporary development events with publish
rules: Label this as a developer testing/debugging tool.
Explain that transient publish events are not stored for replay.
Do not present fake connected state as a real server connection.

safety: Hide unless root admin is active.
Never expose root admin key after entry.

tables: Use sticky headers for data-heavy pages.
Keep row height compact but readable.
Provide clear loading, empty, error, and permission-denied states.
Keep row actions stable so hover states do not resize rows.
Use horizontal scroll for wide data instead of squeezing text into unreadable cells.
forms: Every input needs an accessible label.
Validate field names and JSON before submit when possible.
Disable submit while requests are in flight.
Show exact API error code and recovery guidance.
Use literal primary action labels such as Create index, Save schema, Retry delivery, and Create key.
json_editors: Show line and column parse errors when possible.
Provide format, copy, and reset actions where useful.
dangerous_actions: Use typed confirmation for database and collection deletion.
permission_denied: Disable unavailable actions with a tooltip or helper text naming the missing capability.
Show the current token scope and capabilities.
Do not recommend root admin keys for normal app code.
Keep explanatory content visible when it helps users understand what access is missing.
secrets: Provide copy-once flows for generated tokens and webhook secrets.
Never show old secrets after the dialog closes.
Never include secrets in URLs, logs, screenshots, or persisted UI debug data.

rules: Use exact product terms: database, collection, document, ETag, index, FTS, schema, webhook, operation, capability.
Avoid internal placeholder text such as mock, fixture, demo, or localhost backup names in user-facing dropdowns.
Use helper text only where it prevents mistakes.
Prefer recovery advice over generic failure text.
Do not use marketing copy inside the dashboard.

error_copy_examples.query_limit_exceeded: This query hit a server safety limit. Lower the page size, narrow the filter, avoid deep offset, or create an index for the filtered field.
error_copy_examples.missing_capability: This token cannot manage indexes. Required capability: indexes:manage.
error_copy_examples.schema_validation_failed: This document does not match the collection schema. Fix the highlighted fields or update the schema.
error_copy_examples.webhook_blocked: This webhook target is blocked by SSRF protection. Use a public HTTPS endpoint.
error_copy_examples.etag_mismatch: This document changed since you loaded it. Refresh the document and apply your changes again.

requirements: All controls must be reachable by keyboard.
All form inputs must have labels.
Icon-only buttons must have accessible labels and tooltips.
Color must not be the only indicator of status.
Text contrast should meet WCAG AA.
Dialogs must trap focus and return focus to the trigger.
Tables need clear headers and screen-reader labels for row actions.
Static elements must not be used as buttons.

responsive_behavior.desktop: Use sidebar plus main workspace and optional details drawer.
responsive_behavior.tablet: Collapse secondary panels into drawers and keep tables horizontally scrollable.
responsive_behavior.mobile: Prioritize read and inspect flows. Editing JSON should use a full-screen editor.
responsive_behavior.minimum_width_rule: No text or controls should overlap. Toolbars must wrap or move secondary actions into menus.

frontend: Cancel stale requests when switching database or collection.
Use optimistic UI only where server consistency is clear.
Avoid heavy chart libraries unless the metrics screen truly needs them.
Keep skeletons dimensionally stable to avoid layout shift.
api_usage: Show query budget errors with advice.
Use async management endpoints when available for index and FTS builds.
Do not poll realtime data when an SSE subscription is active.
Preserve ETag headers and send If-Match for edits when available.

must_do: Drive navigation and enabled actions from scope and capabilities.
Keep admin-only features separate from project-owner features.
Store runtime keys and management keys separately in UI state.
Redact secrets after initial copy opportunities.
Display exact operation state for async work.
Map every live control to a real jsonvault-core endpoint or mark it clearly as not connected.
must_not_do: Do not show controls for unsupported core features as if they work.
Do not put the root admin key in frontend runtime code.
Do not use gradients, orbs, decorative blobs, or marketing hero sections.
Do not show fake charts, fake activity, or fake performance claims as real data.
Do not silently retry destructive operations.
Do not claim index or FTS work is complete until the API reports ready.
Do not leak JWTs, webhook secrets, or admin keys into localStorage unless a deliberate threat model approves it.

visual: Dashboard reads as a calm developer console.
Text fits buttons, cells, modals, and sidebars at mobile and desktop widths.
Status colors are semantic and restrained.
No nested cards or decorative floating sections.
product_scope: Every visible live feature maps to jsonvault-core endpoints or documented dashboard-layer auth.
Unsupported follow-ups are not presented as working features.
Runtime, project management, and root admin credentials are clearly separated.
Realtime is described as app live-update testing, not a general chat product.
safety: Missing permissions show the required capability.
Generated secrets are copied once and then redacted.
developer_experience: Errors show code, message, reason, and recovery advice when available.
Document editor validates JSON before submit.
Query UI explains filters, sorting, search, limit, and offset.
Index and FTS screens explain performance tradeoffs plainly.
Copy actions are available for IDs, ETags, API paths, tokens, and webhook signing examples.
verification: No user-facing text uses internal mock labels.
Accessibility lint passes for labels and interactive controls.
